# feat(kaizen-agents): pre-hydrate tool registry from user query (#579)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `2d9df3be4250` — feat(kaizen-agents): pre-hydrate tool registry from user query (#579)

**Body**:

Issue #579 documented that kaizen_agents.delegate.loop.ToolRegistry's
two-phase tool exposure costs every Delegate user 2 of max_turns per
request on search_tools meta-tool calls, before any real data tool
dispatches. On an 8-turn budget that's 25% overhead on every request;
on complex chains it hits the budget ceiling before synthesis.
Reproduced across 8 distinct capability tests on Iris/ImpactVerse.

Root cause: ToolHydrator starts the LLM with a small default tool set
+ search_tools meta-tool. Deferred tools only become invokable after
search_tools(query) hydrates them. This solves the context-window
bloat problem but fragments multi-tool chains across
discovery-before-dispatch.

Fix (Option 1 + Option 4 from the analysis in
workspaces/issue-579-tool-registry-discovery/01-analysis/recommendation.md):

1. ToolHydrator.pre_hydrate_from_query(query, top_k=5)
   - BM25 retrieval over the existing deferred-tool index
   - Seeded with the user's own input (not an LLM classification)
   - Results merged into the active set via existing hydrate()
   - No-op when hydrator is inactive / query is empty / BM25 no match

2. AgentLoop.run_turn invokes pre_hydrate_from_query once per turn
   BEFORE the first _stream_completion. Active-hydrator only.

3. Default system prompt gains a paragraph telling the LLM it can
   batch search_tools + real_tool in one tool-call batch when the
   target name is known (Option 4).

LLM-first compatibility (rules/agent-reasoning.md):
Pre-hydration is retrieval, not routing or classification. The
framework runs BM25 scoring and merges results into the visible
tool set. The LLM still decides whether to invoke any hydrated tool,
which one, and how. search_tools remains available as the escape
hatch for queries the BM25 pre-hydrate misses.

Behavior change: common-case tool-call latency drops by ~600ms and
turn-budget consumption drops by 25%. Token cost per turn increases
slightly (~600 tokens of hydrated tool schemas), amortized over
session length.

Tests (9 new, all Tier 1):
- TestPreHydrateFromQuery: 7 tests covering top-K BM25 hits, top_k
  cap, inactive-hydrator no-op, empty-query no-op, no-match no-op,
  active-set idempotence, search_tools escape-hatch preservation
- TestAgentLoopCallsPreHydrate: 2 tests covering AgentLoop.run_turn
  invocation on active hydrator + skip on inactive hydrator

Full test file 49/49 pass.

kaizen-agents 0.9.3 -> 0.9.4.

Cross-SDK: follow-up check for kailash-rs DefaultToolHydrator parity
per rules/cross-sdk-inspection.md MUST Rule 1.

Closes #579.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
